title: Individual and household survey information | Stats NZ
content:
## Individual and household survey information
Have you been asked to participate in a Stats NZ survey? Find out why you need to take part, what's involved, what we do with your information, and how to get help.
Tēnā koe. Thank you for taking part.

## Why your information is needed
Your information allows Stats NZ to produce statistics about New Zealand.
People in government, business, iwi, and community and other organisations need this information. They use it to make decisions that will help our economy and improve the lives of New Zealanders.
For example, people who complete the Household Economic Survey contribute information to the consumers price index, which measures New Zealand’s inflation rate. The inflation rate is used to adjust the benefits beneficiaries receive, superannuation for retirees, and the official cash rate.
Your information guides where services are needed – services such as hospitals, kōhanga reo, schools, roads, public transport, and recreational facilities.
Information is on our website for everyone to use.
We count on you infographics show important information we collect on your region.

## The law requires you to take part
The information gathered by Stats NZ individual and household surveys is so important that everyone chosen to take part must do so. Stats NZ surveys are a request under section 23 of the Data and Statistics Act 2022. Legally anyone who is asked to complete the survey has to answer all mandatory questions before the end of the collection period. Failing to answer the questions is an offence under section 76 of the Act and a conviction could lead to a fine of up to $2000 for an individual. If you don't think you can take part in a survey, get in touch to talk about your options. Contact us about a survey
We know how important it is to ensure your information is safe.
Privacy, security, and confidentiality of survey data has information about Stats NZ’s standards for keeping your information private, secure, and confidential.

## What we do with your information
We collect information and analyse it to produce statistics about New Zealand. These statistics give information about topics like employment, how much households spend, and how satisfied people feel with their lives.
We keep your identity confidential and your data safe. Our published statistics and research do not identify individuals, households, or businesses.
We store data securely. If it’s not needed, we destroy it.
We don't share individual and household survey information with any other agencies.
We sometimes link survey data with other data so decision-makers and researchers can use it.
When we do this we make sure it's safe. We remove personal details like names and addresses and group the data in a way that stops individual people being identified.
No other organisation – including the Police or Work and Income – can get information that identifies survey responses from individuals.

## Why you were selected
You and your household have been selected to represent other people in your neighbourhood.
We use a fair statistical method based on physical addresses to select households for each survey. These households make up a sample that represents a group of people.

## What's involved in taking part
If you're selected for one of our surveys, you will receive a letter in the post to let you know. A survey interviewer will then come to your home to arrange a time to interview you.
The interviewer will show their identification card when they arrive. They will have your address, but not your name, phone number, or other contact information.
Most individual and household surveys are completed at your home with one of our interviewers. Follow-up interviews for some surveys are done by phone.
We keep our interviews and forms as short and simple as possible.

## How to identify Stats NZ staff
You can identify a Stats NZ survey interviewer by checking they are:
- wearing a Stats NZ photo identification card – check the Certificate of Authority on the back
- wearing a vest, jacket, or other clothing with a Stats NZ logo
- may be driving a car with a Stats NZ logo on it.
Our interviewers will never ask for your bank or credit card details, your Inland Revenue number, or for money.
If you have concerns, please call us toll-free on 0800 809 464 or contact:
- The orb: Report a scam or fraud
- Consumer Protection: Ministry of Consumer Affairs at 04 474 275 or email mcainfo@mca.govt.nz
- your nearest police station.

## 'Do not knock' stickers don't apply
Consumer NZ's 'Do not knock' stickers don't apply to Stats NZ survey interviewers. These stickers are to stop salespeople knocking at your door. Stats NZ interviewers are there to collect information we need to produce official statistics.

## Get information about a specific survey
List of Stats NZ surveys links you to specific information about all the surveys we run.
Get in touch if you:
- can't find the information you need
- need help completing a survey questionnaire
- have a question about a survey
- need to change the date and/or time of your appointment with an interviewer.
Contact us for survey help
